thm:1.1.summary: Integral comparison: for proper smooth formal $\mathfrak{X}/\mathcal{O}_K$, $\mathrm{length}_{\mathbb{Z}_p}(H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(\mathfrak{X}_C, \mathbb{Z}_p)_{\mathrm{tors}}) \le \mathrm{length}_{W(k)}(H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(\mathfrak{X}_k/W(k))_{\mathrm{tors}})$. The headline integrality result: crystalline torsion bounds étale torsion.

thm:1.4.summary: Recovery: when crystalline cohomology is $p$-torsion free, $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(\mathfrak{X}_k/W(k))$ with its $\varphi$-action is functorially recovered from the rigid-analytic generic fibre $X$. Generalizes ordinary $p$-adic Hodge to the integral level.

thm:1.7.summary: $B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$-comparison: for proper smooth adic $X/C$, there are cohomology groups $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(X/B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+)$ with $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(X/B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+) \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}} \cong H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X, \mathbb{Z}_p) \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}}$. The de Rham comparison at the integral level.

thm:1.8.summary: $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-cohomology object: there is a perfect complex $R\Gamma_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}}(\mathfrak{X})$ with a $\varphi$-linear endomorphism specializing to crystalline ($\mathfrak{X}_k$), de Rham (along $\widetilde{\theta}$), and étale (after inverting $\mu$). The single object that interpolates all $p$-adic cohomology theories integrally.

thm:1.10.summary: Master comparison for $A\Omega_{\mathfrak{X}}$: canonical quasi-isomorphisms of sheaves on $\mathfrak{X}_{\mathrm{Zar}}$ recovering crystalline ($\widehat{\otimes} W(k)$), de Rham–Witt ($\widehat{\otimes} \mathcal{O}/p$), Hodge-Tate ($\widehat{\otimes}_{\widetilde{\theta}} \mathcal{O}$), and étale ($\otimes \mathbb{Z}_p$ after inverting). Sheaf-level form of thm:1.8.

thm:1.17.summary: On the torus $R = \mathcal{O}\langle T^{\pm 1}\rangle$, $A\Omega_R$ is computed by the $q$-de Rham complex $A_{\mathrm{inf}}\langle T^{\pm 1}\rangle \xrightarrow{\partial_q/\partial_q T} A_{\mathrm{inf}}\langle T^{\pm 1}\rangle$ with $q = [\epsilon]$. The local model that motivates $q$-de Rham as the right specialization.

thm:2.1.summary: Counterexample: a smooth projective surface $X/\mathbb{Z}_2$ with $\mathbb{Z}_2$-free étale $H^i$ but with $H^2_{\mathrm{crys}}(X_{\overline{\mathbb{F}}_2}/W(\overline{\mathbb{F}}_2))$ having $2$-torsion. Shows the asymmetric inequality of thm:1.1 is sharp — étale-torsion-free does not imply crystalline-torsion-free.

thm:4.28.summary: Fargues's classification: over algebraically closed $C$ of characteristic $0$, finite free Breuil–Kisin–Fargues modules are equivalent to pairs $(T, \Xi)$ of a finite free $\mathbb{Z}_p$-module $T$ and a $B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$-lattice $\Xi$ in $T \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}}$. The classification of the algebraic structure $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-cohomology lives in.

thm:5.1.summary: Scholze's $p$-adic Hodge: $H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X_C, \mathbb{Z}_p)$ is finitely generated, with a comparison isomorphism $H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}} \cong H^i_{\mathrm{dR}}(X) \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}}$. Imported from prior Scholze work; the rational starting point BMS1 strengthens integrally.

thm:8.3.summary: Hodge-Tate sheaf computation: $H^i(\widetilde{\Omega}_{\mathfrak{X}}) \cong \Omega^{i,\mathrm{cont}}_{\mathfrak{X}/\mathcal{O}}\{-i\}$ on $\mathfrak{X}_{\mathrm{Zar}}$, where $\widetilde{\Omega} = L\eta_{\zeta_p-1}(R\nu_* \widehat{\mathcal{O}}^+_X)$. The Hodge-Tate decomposition at the integral level.

thm:9.2.summary: Base change of $A\Omega$: the natural map $A\Omega_{\mathfrak{X}} \otimes^{\mathbb{L}}_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}, \widetilde{\theta}} \mathcal{O} \to L\eta_{\zeta_p-1}(R\nu_* \widehat{\mathcal{O}}^+_X) = \widetilde{\Omega}_{\mathfrak{X}}$ is a quasi-isomorphism, generalized to higher $r$. Mechanism for specializing $A\Omega$ to Hodge-Tate.

thm:10.7.summary: Langer–Zink: there is an initial $F$-$V$-procomplex $\{W_r\Omega^\bullet_{B/A}\}_r$ — the relative de Rham–Witt complex. Imported foundation for the de Rham–Witt half of the comparison.

thm:10.13.summary: The map of complexes $\tau: \Omega^\bullet \to W\Omega^\bullet$ is an injective quasi-isomorphism. Imported Langer–Zink result used in the de Rham–Witt comparison chain.

thm:11.1.summary: Truncated Hodge-Tate: for small formally smooth $R/\mathcal{O}$, $H^i(\widetilde{W_r\Omega}_R) \cong W_r\Omega^{i,\mathrm{cont}}_{R/\mathcal{O}}\{-i\}$. Hodge-Tate computation at level $r$.

thm:12.1.summary: Crystalline comparison: $A\Omega_{\mathfrak{X}} \widehat{\otimes}_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}} A_{\mathrm{crys}} \simeq Ru_* \mathcal{O}^{\mathrm{crys}}_{Y/A_{\mathrm{crys}}}$ in $D(\mathfrak{X}_{\mathrm{Zar}})$. Bridges $A\Omega$ to crystalline cohomology over $A_{\mathrm{crys}}$.

thm:13.1.summary: Existence of $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(X/B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+)$ with the de Rham–étale comparison $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}} \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}} \cong H^i_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \otimes B_{\mathrm{dR}}$. Body proof of thm:1.7.

thm:14.1.summary: Full $A\Omega$ comparison theorem: canonical quasi-isomorphisms with crystalline ($\widehat{\otimes} W(k)$, recovers $W\Omega^\bullet_{\mathfrak{X}_k/W(k)}$), de Rham ($\widehat{\otimes}_{\widetilde{\theta}} \mathcal{O}$), Hodge-Tate, and étale. Body proof of thm:1.10.

thm:14.3.summary: Perfectness: $R\Gamma_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}}(\mathfrak{X}) = R\Gamma(\mathfrak{X}, A\Omega_{\mathfrak{X}})$ is a perfect complex of $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-modules with $\varphi$-linear $\varphi$. Body proof of thm:1.8.

thm:14.5.summary: Torsion comparison: $H^i_{\mathrm{crys}}(\mathfrak{X}_{\mathcal{O}/p}/A_{\mathrm{crys}}) \otimes \dots$ specializes to crystalline / de Rham / étale, with explicit length inequalities recovering thm:1.1. The proof of the integrality main result.

edges.note: Integrality bound is the body theorem thm:14.5 specialized to $A_{\mathrm{crys}}$ side.
Recovery of crystalline cohomology comes from the master comparison of thm:14.1.
$B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$-comparison statement is the intro form of thm:13.1.
Perfectness / $\varphi$-action stated in intro is the body theorem.
Master $A\Omega$ comparison in intro is the body theorem thm:14.1.
Hodge-Tate specialization of $A\Omega$ is the input to the master comparison.
Crystalline specialization runs through $A_{\mathrm{crys}}$ comparison.
Perfectness uses the comparison structure to control the complex's homotopy type.
Length comparison is read off from the master comparison after specialization.
Perfect complex structure controls torsion.
Base change to $\mathcal{O}$ recovers the Hodge-Tate sheaf computation.
Truncated Hodge-Tate computation is the local input limited to level $r=1$.
Relative de Rham–Witt complex is the model the truncated Hodge-Tate is compared against.
Injective quasi-isomorphism input from Langer–Zink.

Theorem 12.1 (crystalline comparison) and Theorem 14.3 (perfectness of R-Gamma_Ainf) both organize the structure of the A_inf-cohomology complex; they share that machinery rather than 12.1 depending on the later 14.3.
$B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$-comparison reduces to Scholze's earlier rational comparison after inverting $\mu$.
$B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$-cohomology defined via $A\Omega$ specialization.
Local torus computation specializes the master comparison.
Hodge-Tate specialization on the torus matches $q$-de Rham at $q=1$.
Fargues's BKF classification organizes the algebraic data of $R\Gamma_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}}$.
Counterexample shows the asymmetric integrality inequality is sharp and not strengthen-able.

main_results_and_motivation.description: Section 1–2. Statement of the integral comparison results and counterexample motivating their asymmetric form.

A_inf_cohomology_object.description: Sections 8, 9, 14. Construction of $A\Omega_{\mathfrak{X}}$ and $R\Gamma_{A_{\mathrm{inf}}}$ as perfect complexes carrying Frobenius. The central technical object.

specializations_to_classical_theories.description: Sections 5, 12, 13, 14. Specializations of $A\Omega$ recovering crystalline, $B_{\mathrm{dR}}^+$, and de Rham–Witt cohomology — and the integral comparison.

de_Rham_Witt_machinery.description: Sections 10–11. Imported Langer–Zink relative de Rham–Witt complex and its truncated Hodge-Tate identification.

module_classification.description: Section 4. Fargues's classification of Breuil–Kisin–Fargues modules — the algebraic skeleton of $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-cohomology.

Scholze:Perfectoid.title: Perfectoid spaces
Scholze:Perfectoid.role: Foundation: perfectoid covers underlie the local computation of $A\Omega$.

Scholze:PAdicHodge.title: $p$-adic Hodge theory for rigid-analytic varieties
Scholze:PAdicHodge.role: Source of thm:5.1; rational comparison BMS1 strengthens integrally.

LangerZink.title: De Rham–Witt cohomology for a proper and smooth morphism
LangerZink.role: Source of thm:10.7 and thm:10.13 (relative de Rham–Witt machinery).

Fargues.title: Quelques résultats et conjectures concernant la courbe

Illusie:DRW.title: Complexe de de Rham–Witt et cohomologie cristalline
